title: Disable automatic file searching
body:
Currently if one of your SV source files contains a module:

```
module top;
   foo u_foo;
endmodule;
```

Verilator will automatically search for `foo`, `foo.v` and `foo.sv` in all the include paths. It outputs something like this:

```
%Error: our.v:3:6: Cannot find file containing module: 'foo'
    3 |      foo u_foo;
      |      ^~~
%Error: our.v:3:6: This may be because there's no search path specified with -I<dir>.
    3 |      foo u_foo;
      |      ^~~
        ... Looked in:
             foo
             foo.v
             foo.sv
             obj_dir/foo
             obj_dir/foo.v
             obj_dir/foo.sv
```

Is there any way to turn this behaviour off? I still need to have search paths for some `include` directives so "don't use `-I` isn't a possible answer unfortunately.

Btw there doesn't seem to be any documentation about this feature, or how SV compilation units work in general (are all the files a single compilation unit?)
